summary: Backend-only testing of Job Pipeline API. Built a comprehensive pytest suite (21 tests) hitting the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL with Bearer session_token auth (sessions seeded directly in MongoDB per /app/auth_testing.md). Discovered and fixed a critical env-loading order bug that broke EVERY Claude-backed endpoint (scoring + content generation). After the fix, all 21 tests pass: health, auth (+401), logout, jobs scrape/dedup/CRUD/status/cascade-delete, prospects, campaign generate (email+linkedin+validation) -> send -> followup, cover-letter (Sonnet + mock GCS), skills aggregate/list, resume CRUD with is_default uniqueness, scheduler status/run, dashboard summary, and cross-user isolation.

critical.issue: FIXED. ai_service.py read EMERGENT_LLM_KEY at module import time, but server.py imported ai_service BEFORE calling load_dotenv(). Result: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY was empty string, emergent proxy detection failed, litellm fell through to direct Anthropic and 500'd with 'Missing Anthropic API Key'. Every Claude-backed endpoint returned 500.

minor.issue: Returns 200 with deleted=0 instead of 404 when the job doesn't exist for the user (or was created by another user). Not a security hole (data is filtered by user_id) but it leaks no signal vs. a successful delete and is inconsistent with GET/POST-status (which 404). Consider returning 404 when deleted_count == 0.

Only honors the session_token cookie; an Authorization: Bearer <token> request to /logout is a no-op because the handler never reads the Authorization header (unlike _get_user_from_request). Tools/tests using Bearer can't log out via the API.

Looks up prospect with db.prospects.find_one({'id': camp['prospect_id']}) WITHOUT user_id filter. Same in /campaigns/{id}/followup and scheduler_service.run_followup_sweep for jobs/prospects lookups. Today this is harmless because the campaign itself is user-scoped, but if prospect_id ever collided across users the email would go to the wrong address. Recommend adding user_id to those find_one filters defensively.

scheduler_run() invokes run_followup_sweep across ALL users (not scoped to the caller). The endpoint currently only requires auth, not admin. Acceptable for an MVP/test trigger, but the contract is misleading - rename to /api/admin/scheduler/run or scope to current user.

action_items: Fix applied to /app/backend/ai_service.py (load_dotenv at module top + lazy _llm_key()). Verify this approach is acceptable; alternatively move `load_dotenv` above `import ai_service` in server.py.
Consider returning 404 from DELETE /api/jobs/{id} when deleted_count == 0 (consistency).
Make /api/auth/logout also honor the Authorization: Bearer header (mirror _get_user_from_request token resolution).
Add user_id scoping to prospect/job find_one calls inside /campaigns/send, /campaigns/{id}/followup, and scheduler_service.run_followup_sweep.
Restrict /api/scheduler/run to admin or scope to the current user (it currently sweeps across all users).
critical_code_review_comments: BUG (fixed): server.py imports ai_service before calling load_dotenv -> EMERGENT_LLM_KEY captured as ''. Always call load_dotenv before importing modules that read env at import time, or use lazy reads.
scheduler_service.run_followup_sweep is global across users; this is reachable through an authenticated POST /api/scheduler/run. Either gate by admin or scope to the caller.

Cover-letter handler hard-codes followup_done=True at creation; correct intent but the field's semantics (was a follow-up sent?) overload the boolean. A dedicated `eligible_for_followup` flag would be cleaner.
ai_service._extract_json regex is non-greedy enough for arrays inside arrays but assumes the model never returns multiple top-level JSON values - acceptable but document.

DELETE /api/resumes/{id} does not 404 on missing resume and does not auto-promote another resume to default if the deleted one was default - users could end up with no default resume even though others exist.
No rate-limiting on Claude-backed endpoints; a malicious authenticated user could burn the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget by repeatedly POST /api/jobs/scrape or /campaigns/generate.
test_credentials: Per /app/auth_testing.md - tests seed users + session_tokens directly into Mongo (test_database.users / user_sessions) and call APIs with Authorization: Bearer <token>. No real Google OAuth used.
seed_data_creation: Two ephemeral users (primary + secondary) created per pytest session and cleaned up in fixture teardown (jobs/prospects/campaigns/skills/resumes/users/user_sessions). Resumes prefixed TEST_.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL: https://talentpath-app.preview.emergentagent.com. Auth via Bearer session_token (seed via Mongo as in /app/auth_testing.md). All Claude calls go through the Emergent proxy via emergentintegrations - the proxy is selected ONLY when api_key starts with 'sk-emergent-', so any future module that reads EMERGENT_LLM_KEY at import time must ensure load_dotenv ran first. Frontend was NOT tested in this iteration. Test suite at /app/backend/tests/ is reusable - rerun with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v.
rca of the issue: RCA: server.py executes `import ai_service` at line 22, BEFORE `load_dotenv(ROOT_DIR / '.env')` at line 29. ai_service.py reads `EMERGENT_LLM_KEY = os.environ.get('EMERGENT_LLM_KEY', '')` at module top, so the constant is bound to '' before .env is loaded. emergentintegrations.llm.chat.LlmChat._is_emergent_key('') returns False, so litellm bypasses the Emergent proxy and tries calling Anthropic directly, which 500s with 'Missing Anthropic API Key'. Repro: any Claude-backed endpoint (jobs/scrape, campaigns/generate, cover-letter, skills/aggregate, campaigns/{id}/followup) returns 500. Fix applied: ai_service.py now (a) calls load_dotenv on its own .env path at import and (b) reads EMERGENT_LLM_KEY lazily via _llm_key() inside _chat(), making it robust to import order. After backend restart, all 21 tests pass.
